_info: W4GGJ Mission Control — HOME STATION (Yaesu FT-450). This dashboard runs on a SEPARATE PC from the station PC where WSJT-X runs.
subtitle: HOME STATION · HF · FT8
_pskreporter_note: Public-page PSKReporter map. Leave blank to auto-build (signals SENT by your callsign, last 15 min). To pin an exact view (bands/modes/time + all Display Options), set up pskreporter.info how you like it, click Permalink, and paste that full URL here.
_wsjtx_note: Agent runs on the station PC alongside WSJT-X. In WSJT-X Settings > Reporting set UDP Server 127.0.0.1 (localhost) and UDP Server port 2242. The agent binds 0.0.0.0:2242 to receive it.
_wsjtx_multicast_note: Only if WSJT-X uses a multicast address (e.g. 239.255.0.0). Leave blank for normal unicast to this PC's IP.
_section_cloner: === UDP CLONER + WEB LOGGER BRIDGE (folds the standalone udp_cloner into this agent) ===
_cloner_note: WSJT-X can only send its UDP stream to ONE destination. Turn this on to run the cloner in the same process as the agent: point WSJT-X at cloner_local_port (2235) and it clones a verbatim copy to every target below PLUS the dashboard engine (wsjtx_udp_port, added automatically) — so one WSJT-X port feeds your other apps AND this dashboard. cloner_adif_path blank = no local ADIF file (QRZ stays the logbook). cloner_blog_folder blank = park-ref watcher off. callsign/grid above are reused for the web-logger Type-5 packets.
_section_adif: === LOGBOOK (ADIF) — full history lives on the STATION PC ===
_adif_note: Cross-PC options: (A) share the station PC's WSJT-X folder and put its UNC path here, e.g. \\STATION-PC\Users\NAME\AppData\Local\WSJT-X\wsjtx_log.adi  -> FULL history. (B) leave blank with adif_autodetect=false -> the dashboard builds a LIVE log straight from WSJT-X UDP as you log each QSO (this session).
_qrz_note: Read-only pull of your FULL QRZ logbook (all history + every new contact your apps upload, any mode). The secret API KEY goes in agent.config.json (gitignored) as "qrz_api_key" or the QRZ_API_KEY env var — NOT here. When a key is set QRZ drives the logbook and the local WSJT-X ADIF is ignored. Sync is incremental: the whole book loads once, then qrz_sync_sec only pulls records newer than the highest log id held (tiny). qrz_full_sync_sec re-downloads the whole book occasionally to catch edits/deletions (which keep their old id).
_section_rigctld: === HAMLIB rigctld (optional — live power / SWR / ALC / meters) ===
_rigctld_note: Set rigctld_enabled=true to stream live rig telemetry (actual power, SWR, ALC, S-meter, drain V/I, temp — whatever your rig reports; probed on connect and logged). The agent reads from rigctld here; point rigctld at whatever controls the radio. (A) HRD/WSJT-X->HRD: 'rigctld -m <HRD model, see: rigctl --list | findstr /i deluxe> -r 127.0.0.1:7809' (HRD allows multiple clients, runs alongside WSJT-X). (B) WSJT-X drives the rig directly: 'rigctld -m 1023 -r COMx -s 38400' (1023=FT-450) then set WSJT-X Rig='Hamlib NET rigctl' server 127.0.0.1:4532. Freq/mode already come via WSJT-X UDP regardless. rig_max_power_watts converts the rig's 0-100% power level to watts.
_section_hrd: === HAM RADIO DELUXE (direct — live power / gains / SWR flag) ===
_hrd_note: For stations where HRD controls the rig and WSJT-X points at HRD (so rigctld can't reach the CAT port). Set hrd_enabled=true to read live data straight from HRD's TCP interface — real RF power, gains (Mic/RF/NR), and a High-SWR warning appear on both dashboards. No Hamlib needed; HRD must be running with Rig Control connected. HRD does NOT expose the numeric PWR/SWR/ALC bar values. To also get those numeric meters (S-meter/SWR/ALC), run rigctld alongside HRD and set rigctld_enabled=true as well — the two complement each other (HRD = power/gains/High-SWR flag, rigctld = numeric meters). Both are enabled here for the W4GGJ station. rig_max_power_watts scales the 0-100 power level to watts.
_section_flrig: === FLRIG (rig control app that DOES expose the meters) ===
_flrig_note: Flrig controls the FT-450 and publishes frequency, mode, PTT and — unlike HRD — the REAL S-meter, power-out and SWR over XML-RPC (default port 12345). Run Flrig as your rig controller instead of HRD, then set flrig_enabled=true and turn hrd_enabled + rigctld_enabled OFF (one rig source at a time). In Flrig enable meter polling (Config > Setup > Polling: check s-meter/power/swr). Point WSJT-X at Rig='FLRig FLRig' (127.0.0.1). Gives a live S-meter on voice AND digital.
_sdr_note: Optional. A separate process (sdr_agent.py) drives an SDR as a band scope that FOLLOWS the radio: it reads the live dial frequency, tunes the SDR to match, FFTs, and feeds the in-shack /console page a live spectrum + waterfall. Preset for a Nooelec NESDR SMArt v5 (RTL2832U) on HF: rtlsdr driver + direct sampling (Q branch) — the only way a plain RTL-SDR hears 40m; center = dial (no upconverter). Steps: (1) pip install numpy pyrtlsdr (Windows: also the rtl-sdr DLLs / Zadig WinUSB driver). (2) set sdr_enabled=true. (3) run 'python sdr_agent.py' on the PC with the SDR (or 'python sdr_agent.py --synthetic' to preview the console with fake data first). sdr_engine_url points at the dashboard server. Lower sdr_sample_rate for a tighter band view (valid RTL rates ~250000 or 900001-2560000).
_sdr_relay_note: Remote waterfall: set sdr_relay_cloud=true to also push a decimated spectrum to the cloud so /console shows a live waterfall away from home. It reuses cloud_url + ingest_token from agent.config.json (never put the token here). sdr_relay_bins/sdr_relay_fps keep the cloud + viewer bandwidth small (256 bins @ 4 fps ~ tiny). The LAN /console still gets the full-res frame.
_dxcluster_note: The DX Cluster panel streams live spots from a telnet cluster node (dxsummit.fi's HTTP API is unreachable from the cloud host). Point at any public node. It logs in with the station callsign. Override without a redeploy via Render env vars DX_CLUSTER_HOST / DX_CLUSTER_PORT if the default node is unreachable. Alternatives: dxc.ve7cc.net:23, dxfun.com:8000, cluster.dl9gtb.de:8000.
